pattern: A sliding window that allows at most k zeros inside it grows greedily and only contracts when the zero budget is exceeded, giving the longest flippable run.
hints: Reframe the goal as the longest window containing no more than k zeros, since those zeros are the ones you flip.
Extend a right edge across the array, counting zeros, and pull the left edge forward whenever there are too many zeros inside.
Maintain left and right bounds and a zero counter; advance right always, and while the window holds more than k zeros advance left dropping its zeros, tracking the maximum width seen.

edge_inputs.description: three flips inside a longer array
zero budget means longest existing run only
budget covers all zeros so whole array qualifies
single zero with no budget splits the run

stress.description: large random binary array with varied flip budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 4000))
    nums = [1 if rng.random() < 0.6 else 0 for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, n)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: majority ones with random zeros exercises window growth and shrink

oracle_python:
import bisect
class Solution:
    def longestOnes(self, nums, k):
        zero_pos = [i for i, v in enumerate(nums) if v == 0]
        n = len(nums)
        if k >= len(zero_pos):
            return n
        best = 0
        for start in range(len(zero_pos) - k + 1):
            left = zero_pos[start - 1] + 1 if start > 0 else 0
            end_zero_idx = start + k
            right = zero_pos[end_zero_idx] - 1 if end_zero_idx < len(zero_pos) else n - 1
            best = max(best, right - left + 1)
        return best
